history.qwen_observation: Tests fail with Unsupported class file major version 65 because Spring Boot 3.0.1 bundles an ASM version that cannot parse Java 21 bytecode.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading Spring Boot to 3.3 before bumping the JDK target ensures the framework's bundled ASM version supports Java 21 class files, resolving the BeanDefinitionStoreException during test context initialization.

history.qwen_observation: Integration tests regressed after applying the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade recipe, likely due to dependency version shifts rather than JDK bytecode incompatibility, as tests passed on JDK 21 pre-recipe.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the aggressive Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step and reverting to the default Java 17-to-21 migration chain avoids unnecessary dependency churn. Since tests already passed on JDK 21 before recipes, the framework version bump was the probable cause of the regression. The default chain focuses solely on plugin and build configuration updates required for JDK 21.

history.qwen_observation: Tests fail with ASM ClassReader 'Unsupported class file major version 65' — Spring Boot 3.0.1 bundles an ASM version that cannot parse Java 21 bytecode (class file version 65).
history.qwen_rationale: The project is already on Spring Boot 3.x (3.0.1), but that version predates Java 21 and bundles an older ASM that can't read class file version 65. Upgrading to Spring Boot 3.3 (which supports Java 21 per the compat matrix) brings in a newer ASM that can parse Java 21 bytecode. The library match's fix_snippet (UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3) is the correct fix even though its diagnosis mentions SB-2 — the root cause is identical: ASM version too old for the target bytecode. I prepend this step before the Java 21 build upgrade so the framework is ready before we compile to Java 21.

history.qwen_observation: Integration tests regressed after applying Java 21 code transforms, likely due to semantic changes in SequencedCollection (getFirst vs get(0) exception types) or UseLocaleOf altering runtime behavior.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing aggressive Java 21 code transforms that modify method calls or exception semantics, which frequently cause integration test failures. Retaining only safe, non-semantic transforms and the core JDK/framework upgrades ensures Java 21 compatibility without breaking test expectations.
